sanskrit: निमित्तानि च पश्यामि विपरीतानि केशव । न च श्रेयोऽनुपश्यामि हत्वा स्वजनमाहवे ॥
hindiTranslation: हे केशव मैं लक्षणों  शकुनों को भी विपरीत देख रहा हूँ और युद्ध में स्वजनोंको मारकर श्रेय लाभ भी नहीं देख रहा हूँ ॥
englishTranslation: O Keshava, I see only inauspicious signs, and I see no good in killing my own people in battle.
essence: A victory that destroys your own people is not worth having.
speaker: Arjuna
simpleMeaning: Arjuna says he sees bad signs and no real good in killing his own relatives in battle.
simpleInsight: When the heart is already broken, even victory can look empty.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna stands frozen before the two armies. After feeling his body fail and his mind shake, he tells Krishna that the signs around him feel wrong and that killing his own kin cannot lead to anything good.
modernRelevance: You are about to send the message that will end a friendship, and every detail feels wrong. The mind keeps warning: forcing this will not make life better.
translationLiteral: निमित्तानि (signs) / च (and) / पश्यामि (I see) / विपरीतानि (opposite, inauspicious) / केशव (O Keshava) / न (not) / च (and) / श्रेयः (good, welfare) / अनुपश्यामि (I see) / हत्वा (having killed) / स्वजनम् (my own people) / आहवे (in battle)
insight: It shifts attention from winning to the moral cost of winning.
